description: STING standard drawing notes — General + per-discipline. Corporate baseline; projects may override or extend per discipline key at <project>/_BIM_COORD/discipline_notes.json (project wins by key). Rendered by NotesBlockCommand and the DrawingProducer Notes production path.

General.title: GENERAL NOTES
notes: Do not scale from this drawing. Use figured dimensions only; report any discrepancies to the design team before proceeding.
All dimensions are in millimetres and all levels in metres to project datum unless noted otherwise.
This drawing is to be read in conjunction with all relevant architectural, structural and services drawings and specifications.
All work is to comply with the current Building Regulations, applicable British/EN Standards and the project specification.
Contractor to verify all dimensions and site conditions on site prior to commencing work or fabrication.
Setting out to be agreed with the design team prior to construction.
Report any discrepancy between drawings, specification and site conditions to the design team for resolution before proceeding.
This drawing remains the property of the originator and must not be reproduced without written consent.

A.title: ARCHITECTURAL NOTES
notes: All setting-out dimensions are to structural grid lines or finished faces as indicated.
Refer to door, window and room-finish schedules for ironmongery, glazing and finishes.
Fire-rated construction to be installed strictly in accordance with the fire strategy and manufacturer's certified details.
All junctions and penetrations to maintain the acoustic and fire performance of the element.
Wet-area finishes and falls to gullies to comply with Approved Document / project waterproofing specification.

S.title: STRUCTURAL NOTES
notes: All structural work to comply with the relevant Eurocodes and their UK National Annexes.
Concrete grades, cover and reinforcement to be as shown on the structural drawings and bar schedules.
Do not commence fabrication until drawings are issued for construction and dimensions confirmed on site.
Temporary works and stability during construction are the responsibility of the contractor.
Foundation bearing strata and design bearing pressure to be confirmed on site by the engineer prior to pouring.

M.title: MECHANICAL NOTES
notes: All mechanical installations to comply with the relevant CIBSE guides, Building Regulations Part F/L and the project specification.
Ductwork to be constructed and sealed to DW/144 and the specified pressure/leakage class.
Provide access panels and clear maintenance access to all dampers, valves, coils and in-line equipment.
All ductwork and pipework to be insulated as specified; identify systems with flow direction and service labels.
Commission and balance all systems to the specified design flow rates and record on commissioning sheets.

E.title: ELECTRICAL NOTES
notes: All electrical installations to comply with BS 7671 (current edition) and the project specification.
Cable sizes, protective devices and circuit references are indicated on the drawings and schedules — verify against final loads.
Provide segregation between LV, ELV and data/comms services in accordance with BS 7671 and BS EN 50174.
All final circuits to be tested and certified; issue electrical installation certificates on completion.
Emergency lighting and fire-alarm installations to comply with BS 5266 and BS 5839 respectively.

P.title: PUBLIC HEALTH / PLUMBING NOTES
notes: All above- and below-ground drainage to comply with BS EN 12056 / BS EN 752 and Approved Document H.
Hot and cold water services to comply with BS EN 806, BS 8558 and the Water Supply (Water Fittings) Regulations.
Provide backflow protection appropriate to the fluid category in accordance with BS EN 1717.
Pipework gradients, invert levels and cover depths to be as indicated; confirm connections to existing on site.
Pressure-test and disinfect all water services and record results prior to handover.
